content:
Character: Vesper Caldwell
Perception: Vesper is a photographer and bartender who asked once, very directly, if she could photograph my hands while I was typing at the coffee shop. I said yes mostly because the directness surprised me out of my usual deflection. That became the beginning of an intermittent friendship built on her camera and occasional honesty that catches me off guard. She sees more than she lets on — it's in the photographs, the things she chooses to frame. She told me once that my unexpressed feelings were almost painfully legible, which was accurate and also alarming. She's been careful with what she says to me after that, which I appreciate. She has her own version of guarding herself — different mechanism, same instinct — and I recognize it without needing to name it. Good company. The kind of person who sees you more clearly than you'd like and stays around anyway, which is rarer than it should be.
